content: Let me read the necessary files to complete this lean-vs-blueprint check task.
I'll read the subagent instructions, project context, and then the relevant Lean and blueprint files.

1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

10	/-!
11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in
14	`PresheafCech.lean`; this file owns the two free-complex declarations:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex`  (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`)
17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug : K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism / the free
23	  complex is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.  **(built and axiom-clean.)**  The proof reduces objectwise
24	  (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`) to: the empty case `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty`; and the nonempty
25	  case `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`, which transfers the engine quasi-isomorphism
26	  `cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso` across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`, using the degree-`0` augmentation
27	  identification `cechFreeAug_eval_eq` and the geometric identification
28	  `coverStructurePresheafEval_iso`.
29	
30	The quasi-isomorphism is proved objectwise (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`): for each open `V`,
31	the evaluated augmented complex is the augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full
32	simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with constant coefficients `O_X(V)`, which is
33	contractible when `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (via the prepend-`i_fix` homotopy
34	`FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy`) and zero when `I₁(V) = ∅`.  This file owns the
35	combinatorial engine (`FreeCechEngine`) supplying that contracting homotopy and the
36	per-`V` sectionwise reductions building toward `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`.
37	-/
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
44	
45	/-
46	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
47	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
48	
49	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
51	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
53	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
54	with the alternating-face differential.
55	
56	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
57	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
58	   above direct sums.
59	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
60	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
61	
62	Key API:
63	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
64	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
65	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
66	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
67	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
68	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
69	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
70	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
71	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
72	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
73	
74	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
75	path above.
76	
77	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
79	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
81	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
82	
83	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
84	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
85	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
86	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
87	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
88	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
89	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
90	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
91	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
92	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
93	
94	Key API:
95	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
96	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
97	
98	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
99	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
100	-/
101	
102	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
103	
104	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
105	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
106	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
107	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
108	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
109	
110	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
111	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
112	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
113	
114	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
115	
116	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
117	
118	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
119	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
120	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
121	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
122	
123	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
124	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
125	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
126	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
127	
128	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
129	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
130	
131	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
132	
133	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
134	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
135	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
136	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
137	
138	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
139	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
140	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
141	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
142	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
143	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
144	
145	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
146	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
147	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
148	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
149	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
150	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
151	  subst e; simp
152	
153	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
154	
155	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
156	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
157	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
158	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
159	
160	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
161	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
162	
163	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
164	Mathlib counterpart. -/
165	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
166	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
167	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
168	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
169	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
170	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
171	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
172	  map_id n := by
173	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
174	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
175	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
176	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
177	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
178	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
179	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
180	      eqToHom_map]
181	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
182	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
183	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
184	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
185	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
186	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
187	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
188	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
189	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
190	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
191	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
192	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
193	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
194	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
195	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
196	    congr 2
197	
198	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
199	
200	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
201	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
202	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
203	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
204	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
205	
206	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
207	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
208	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
209	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
210	
211	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
212	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
213	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
214	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
215	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
216	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
217	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
218	  rfl
219	
220	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
221	
222	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
223	
224	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
225	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
226	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
227	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
228	
229	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
230	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
231	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
232	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
233	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
234	
235	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
236	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
237	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
238	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
239	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
240	
241	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
242	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
243	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
244	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
245	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
246	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
247	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
248	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
249	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
250	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
251	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
252	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
253	    congr 1
254	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
255	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
256	  rw [hmap] at hnat
257	  rw [hnat]
258	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
259	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
260	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
261	
262	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
263	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
264	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
265	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
266	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
267	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
268	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
269	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
270	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
271	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
272	
273	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
274	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
275	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
276	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
277	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
278	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
279	
280	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
281	
282	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
283	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
284	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
285	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
286	
287	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
288	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
289	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
290	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
291	
292	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
293	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
294	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
295	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
296	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
297	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
298	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
299	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
300	  intro σ
301	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
302	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
303	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
304	
305	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
306	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
307	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
308	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
309	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
310	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
311	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
312	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
313	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
314	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
315	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
316	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
317	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
318	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
319	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
320	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
321	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
322	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
323	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
324	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
325	    abel
326	  rw [hd]; exact main
327	
328	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
329	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
330	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
331	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
332	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
333	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
334	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
335	    Limits.zero_comp]
336	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰
337	
338	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
339	
340	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
341	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
342	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
343	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
344	
345	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
346	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
347	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
348	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
349	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
350	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
351	
352	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
353	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
354	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
355	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
356	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]
357	
358	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms
359	
360	Homology in `PresheafOfModules R` is computed objectwise: the evaluation functors
361	`PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V` are jointly conservative and preserve homology, so a
362	morphism of complexes of presheaves of modules is a quasi-isomorphism as soon as each of its
363	evaluations is.  These three lemmas package that reduction.  Mathlib has the single-functor
364	statement `HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_map_iff_of_preservesHomology` (which needs the functor to
365	reflect isomorphisms — false for a single evaluation), but not the joint-conservativity version
366	needed to reduce a quasi-isomorphism of presheaf-of-module complexes to its sectionwise checks. -/
367	
368	open HomologicalComplex in
369	/-- For a functor `F` preserving homology, if the induced map on the homology of the mapped
370	complexes is an isomorphism, then `F` applied to the homology map is an isomorphism.  This is the
371	naturality square of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso` read off at a fixed degree. -/
372	private lemma isIso_Fmap_homologyMap {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {C₁ C₂ : Type*}
373	    [Category C₁] [Category C₂] [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive C₂]
374	    {K L : HomologicalComplex C₁ c} (φ : K ⟶ L) (F : C₁ ⥤ C₂) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
375	    (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
376	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).HasHomology i]
377	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj L).HasHomology i]
378	    (hiso : IsIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i)) :
379	    IsIso (F.map (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ i)) := by
380	  have key := ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso_hom_naturality ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ) F
381	  haveI hmid : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
382	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := hiso
383	  change IsIso (F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)))
384	  haveI hcomp : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
385	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)) ≫
386	      (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj L).mapHomologyIso F).hom) := inferInstance
387	  haveI hcomp2 : IsIso ((((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom ≫
388	      F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := key ▸ hcomp
389	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left
390	    (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom _
391	
392	/-- **Joint conservativity of evaluations.** A morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules is an
393	isomorphism as soon as each of its evaluations `(evaluation R V).map g` is, since the underlying
394	presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of presheaves is an
395	isomorphism iff it is so objectwise.  Project-local: the joint-conservativity packaging is not in
396	Mathlib. -/
397	private lemma isIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat)
398	    {M N : PresheafOfModules R} (g : M ⟶ N)
399	    (h : ∀ V, IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).map g)) : IsIso g := by
400	  suffices hh : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g) from
401	    isIso_of_reflects_iso g (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)
402	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
403	  intro V
404	  have hV := h V
405	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map] at hV
406	  haveI : IsIso (g.app V) := hV
407	  have e : ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g).app V
408	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab).map (g.app V) := rfl
409	  rw [e]
410	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab) (g.app V)
411	
412	/-- **Objectwise reduction of quasi-isomorphisms.** A morphism `φ` of chain complexes of
413	presheaves of `R`-modules is a quasi-isomorphism if, for every object `V`, its evaluation
414	`(evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex |>.map φ` (a morphism of complexes of `R(V)`-modules) is a
415	quasi-isomorphism.  This is the formal core of the sectionwise argument that the free Čech complex
416	resolves the cover structure presheaf: homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise.
417	
418	Project-local: combines `isIso_of_evaluation` (joint conservativity) with `isIso_Fmap_homologyMap`
419	(evaluation preserves homology). -/
420	lemma quasiIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat) {ι : Type*}
421	    {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules R) c} (φ : K ⟶ L)
422	    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
423	    (h : ∀ V, QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ)) :
424	    QuasiIso φ := by
425	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
426	  intro i
427	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
428	  apply isIso_of_evaluation R
429	  intro V
430	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map]
431	  haveI hqV : QuasiIsoAt (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i :=
432	    (h V).quasiIsoAt i
433	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap] at hqV
434	  exact isIso_Fmap_homologyMap φ (PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V) i hqV
435	
436	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — combinatorial contracting-homotopy engine
437	
438	The objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` turns `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` into a
439	per-open-`V` statement: the evaluation of the augmented free Čech complex at `V` is the
440	augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with
441	constant coefficients the ring `O_X(V)`.  This section ports the constant-coefficient
442	combinatorial contracting homotopy — the prepend-`i_fix` map and its `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id`
443	identity — into this file as a self-contained algebraic engine.
444	
445	This is the free-side analogue of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` in `CechAcyclic.lean`
446	(those declarations are `private` there, hence unavailable here); the proofs are the same
447	alternating-sum cancellation, specialised to the constant coefficient module.  Here `M` is
448	the constant coefficient module (to be `O_X(V)` in the application) and `ι` the index type
449	(to be `I₁(V)`). -/
450	
451	namespace FreeCechEngine
452	
453	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
454	
455	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
456	`(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`, the alternating sum of the index-dropping
457	maps.  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`. -/
458	def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
459	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
460	
461	/-- The contracting homotopy: prepend the fixed index `r`.  `(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`.
462	Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`. -/
463	def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
464	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
465	
466	@[simp] lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
467	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
468	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
469	
470	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(k+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the `k`-th
471	coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
472	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
473	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
474	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
475	  funext l
476	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
477	  · simp
478	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
479	
480	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on the constant-coefficient
481	Čech complex (Stacks `lemma-homology-complex`, the `dh + hd = id` computation).  Free-side
482	port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`. -/
483	lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
484	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
485	  funext σ
486	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
487	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
488	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
489	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
490	    funext i; simp
491	  rw [h0]
492	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
493	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
494	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
495	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
496	
497	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then `t = d (h t)`. -/
498	lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
499	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
500	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
501	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
502	  exact h
503	
504	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
505	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
506	lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
507	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
508	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
509	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
510	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
511	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
512	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
513	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
514	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
515	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
516	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
517	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
518	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
519	
520	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex, via the
521	sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`. -/
522	lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
523	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
524	  funext σ
525	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
526	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
527	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
528	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
529	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
530	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
531	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
532	      funext k
533	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
534	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
535	    simp only [harg]
536	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
537	    simp
538	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
539	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
540	    intro hj
541	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
542	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
543	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
544	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
545	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
546	
547	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex
548	(`Function.Exact` form).  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact`. -/
549	lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
550	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
551	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
552	  intro x
553	  constructor
554	  · intro hx
555	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
556	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
557	    exact combDifferential_comp y
558	
559	end FreeCechEngine
560	
561	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sectionwise reduction of the free Čech complex -/
562	
563	/-- **Evaluation preserves the degreewise coproduct of the free Čech complex.**
564	
565	The objectwise-reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` requires understanding the evaluation of
566	`cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` at an open `V`.  Since the degree-`p` term is the coproduct
567	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and the evaluation functor
568	preserves finite colimits (`PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`,
569	with `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite as `𝒰.I₀` is finite), evaluating commutes with the
570	coproduct:
571	`(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`.
572	This is the degree-`p` entry point for the sectionwise description
573	(blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`); each evaluated summand
574	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free

710	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — degreewise engine model of the evaluated free complex
711	
712	The nonempty-case analysis of the evaluated free Čech complex needs an explicit degreewise
713	identification of `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p)` with the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
714	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This section builds that
715	degreewise object isomorphism as the composite
716	
717	  `(eval V)(K_p) ≅[cechFreeEval_X] ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I₀} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
718	  `           ≅[drop-zeros]      ∐_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
719	  `           ≅[whiskerEquiv]    ∐_{τ : Fin(p+1)→I₁(V)} O_X(V),`
720	
721	using `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` to discard the summands with `V ≰ U_σ` and
722	`freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` to identify each surviving summand with `O_X(V)`.  The differential
723	match turning this degreewise iso into an iso of chain complexes is the genuine remaining
724	bottleneck and is NOT discharged here. -/
725	
726	/-- `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` holds iff every value of `σ` indexes a cover member containing `V`;
727	i.e. `σ` factors through `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This is the index-splitting criterion separating
728	the surviving summands of the evaluated free Čech complex from the vanishing ones. -/
729	lemma le_coverInterOpen_iff (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
730	    {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
731	    V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ↔ ∀ k, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) := by
732	  simp only [coverInterOpen, le_iInf_iff]
733	
734	/-- **Reindexing the surviving multi-indices.** The multi-indices `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` with
735	`V ≤ U_σ` are in bijection with the maps `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)` into the subtype
736	`I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` of cover members containing `V`.  This bijection feeds
737	`Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` to collapse the
738	surviving coproduct onto the constant-coefficient combinatorial index `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`. -/
739	def survivingEquiv (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
740	    (Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) ≃
741	      {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} where
742	  toFun τ := ⟨fun k => (τ k).1, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V _).2 fun k => (τ k).2⟩
743	  invFun σ := fun k => ⟨σ.1 k, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V σ.1).1 σ.2 k⟩
744	  left_inv τ := by funext k; rfl
745	  right_inv σ := by ext k; rfl
746	
747	/-- **Drop-zeros isomorphism.** Evaluating the degree-`p` free Čech term at `V` and splitting off
748	the vanishing summands: the full coproduct over all `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` is isomorphic to the
749	coproduct over the *surviving* multi-indices `{σ : V ≤ U_σ}`, because every non-surviving summand
750	`(eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)` (with `V ≰ U_σ`) is a zero object
751	(`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`).  This is the first of the two halves identifying the evaluated
752	term with the combinatorial model. -/
753	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalDropZeros (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
754	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
755	    (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
756	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
757	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) ≅
758	      (∐ fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
759	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
760	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) where
761	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => by
762	    classical
763	    exact if h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ then
764	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
765	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
766	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) ⟨σ, h⟩
767	    else 0
768	  inv := Limits.Sigma.desc fun s =>
769	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
770	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
771	        (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) s.1
772	  hom_inv_id := by
773	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
774	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id]
775	    by_cases h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ
776	    · rw [dif_pos h, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
777	    · rw [dif_neg h, Limits.zero_comp]
778	      exact ((freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le h).eq_zero_of_src _).symm
779	  inv_hom_id := by
780	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro s
781	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, dif_pos s.2]
782	
783	/-- **Degreewise engine model of the evaluated free Čech term.** The degree-`p` term of the
784	evaluated free Čech complex at `V` is isomorphic to the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
785	`∐_{τ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  Built as the composite of
786	`cechFreeEval_X` (evaluation commutes with the coproduct), `cechFreeEvalDropZeros` (discard the
787	`V ≰ U_σ` summands), and `Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` along `survivingEquiv` with the per-summand
788	identification `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`.  This is the object half of the engine iso
789	`cechFreeEvalEngineIso`; the differential match remains. -/
790	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalEngine_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
791	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
792	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
793	        ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p) ≅
794	      ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>

796	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
797	  cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p ≪≫ cechFreeEvalDropZeros 𝒰 V p ≪≫

877	/-- **The engine chain complex** `C•` of an open `V` meeting some cover member.
878	
879	The chain complex of `O_X(V)`-modules with `C_p = ∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)` and the
880	alternating index-dropping differential `cechEngineD`.  It is the constant-coefficient combinatorial
881	model that `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` identifies with the evaluated free Čech complex; the nonempty
882	acyclicity of the evaluated complex reduces to the contractibility of `C•`.
883

885	noncomputable def cechEngineComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
886	    ChainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) ℕ :=
887	  ChainComplex.of (cechEngineX 𝒰 V) (cechEngineD 𝒰 V) (cechEngineD_comp 𝒰 V)

889	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — contracting homotopy of the engine complex
890	
891	Fixing an index `i_fix ∈ I₁(V)` (which exists exactly when `V` meets some cover member), the
892	prepend-`i_fix` map `ι_σ ↦ ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}` contracts the engine complex: `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id`
893	in every positive degree.  This is the chain (coproduct) port of `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy` /
894	`combHomotopy_spec`, and it yields the positive-degree exactness of `cechEngineComplex` feeding the
895	nonempty case of the free Čech quasi-isomorphism. -/
896	
897	/-- The prepend-`i_fix` contracting map `C_p ⟶ C_{p+1}` of the engine complex: on the coproduct
898	injection `ι_σ` (`σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`) it returns `ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}`. -/
899	noncomputable def cechEnginePrepend (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
900	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (p : ℕ) :
901	    cechEngineX 𝒰 V p ⟶ cechEngineX 𝒰 V (p + 1) :=
902	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
903	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
904	      (Fin.cons i_fix σ)
905	
906	/-- Action of the prepend map on a coproduct injection. -/
907	lemma cechEnginePrepend_ι (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
908	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (p : ℕ)
909	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) :
910	    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix p
911	      = Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
912	          coverSectionModule V) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) := by
913	  simp only [cechEnginePrepend, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
914	
915	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id` for the engine complex in positive
916	degree: the coproduct port of `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec`.  The `i = 0` term of `s ≫ d`
917	returns the identity (dropping the just-prepended `i_fix`), and every higher term cancels in pairs
918	against the corresponding term of `d ≫ s` by `FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ`. -/
919	lemma cechEnginePrepend_spec (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
920	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (n : ℕ) :
921	    cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix (n + 1) ≫ cechEngineD 𝒰 V (n + 1)
922	      + cechEngineD 𝒰 V n ≫ cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix n
923	      = 𝟙 (cechEngineX 𝒰 V (n + 1)) := by
924	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
925	  rw [Preadditive.comp_add, Category.comp_id]
926	  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEnginePrepend_ι, cechEngineD_ι]
927	  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ι, Preadditive.sum_comp]
928	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp, cechEnginePrepend_ι]
929	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun i : Fin (n + 3) => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
930	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (n + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
931	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (n + 3) → _) ∘ i.succAbove))]
932	  have h0 : (Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (n + 3) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i})
933	      ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 3)).succAbove = σ := by funext k; simp
934	  rw [h0]
935	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
936	  rw [add_assoc, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
937	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun k _ => by
938	    rw [FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
939	    abel), add_zero]
940	
941	/-- **Positive-degree exactness of the engine complex** (`Function.Exact` form), the coproduct port
942	of `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_exact`.  When `I₁(V)` is nonempty (witnessed by `i_fix`), the
943	engine differential is exact at every positive degree: a cycle `x` with `cechEngineD n x = 0` is the
944	boundary `cechEngineD (n+1) (cechEnginePrepend (n+1) x)`, by the contracting identity
945	`cechEnginePrepend_spec`.  Feeds the nonempty-case homology vanishing via
946	`ModuleCat.shortComplex_exact`. -/
947	lemma cechEngineD_exact (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
948	    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (n : ℕ) :
949	    Function.Exact (cechEngineD 𝒰 V (n + 1)) (cechEngineD 𝒰 V n) := by
950	  intro x
951	  constructor
952	  · intro hx
953	    refine ⟨(cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix (n + 1)) x, ?_⟩
954	    have hspec := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (cechEnginePrepend_spec 𝒰 V i_fix n)
955	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_id] at hspec
956	    have hx2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hspec x
957	    rw [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_apply,
958	      hx, map_zero, add_zero] at hx2
959	    exact hx2
960	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
961	    have hcomp := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (cechEngineD_comp 𝒰 V n)
962	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_zero] at hcomp
963	    have h2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hcomp y
964	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply] at h2
965	    exact h2

988	    change (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique _ _ (V ⟶ W)) (ModuleCat.freeMk g) = 1

990	    simp only [ModuleCat.freeMk, Unique.eq_default g, Finsupp.single_eq_same]

1136	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the engine chain isomorphism
1137	
1138	The differential comm-square `cechFreeEvalEngine_comm` upgrades the degreewise object isos
1139	`cechFreeEvalEngine_X` to an isomorphism of chain complexes between the evaluation of the free Čech
1140	complex at `V` and the constant-coefficient engine complex `cechEngineComplex`.  This is the chain
1141	iso the nonempty-case quasi-isomorphism is transferred across. -/
1142	
1143	/-- **The engine chain isomorphism** (`lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso`).  For each open `V`, the
1144	evaluation at `V` of the free Čech complex is isomorphic, as a chain complex, to the
1145	constant-coefficient engine complex `cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V`.  Built from the degreewise object isos
1146	`cechFreeEvalEngine_X` and the differential comm-square `cechFreeEvalEngine_comm`.
1147	
1148	Project-local: the identification of the evaluated free Čech complex with its combinatorial
1149	model has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
1150	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalEngineIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1151	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
1152	    ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex
1153	        (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).obj (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰) ≅ cechEngineComplex 𝒰 V :=

1155	    intro i j hij

1448	    exact cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty 𝒰 W h
