1451	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — family-parameterized free Čech resolution
1452	
1453	Re-parameterization of the entire free Čech machinery from `(𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]`
1454	to a raw finite family of opens `{ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (U : ι → Opens X)`, with NO covering
1455	hypothesis.  Mechanically substitutes `𝒰.I₀ ↦ ι`, `coverOpen 𝒰 ↦ U`, `coverInterOpen 𝒰 ↦
1456	coverInterOpenFam U`.  The open-indexed building blocks (`freeYoneda`, `freeYonedaAug`,
1457	`freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`, …) and the generic engine (`FreeCechEngine`, `quasiIso_of_evaluation`)
1458	are reused unchanged.  Delivers the cover-agnostic `cechFreeComplexFam_quasiIso` consumed by the
1459	family-form `injective_cech_acyclic`.  The `X.OpenCover`-named declarations above are kept
1460	byte-identical (so `CechBridge.lean` stays green). -/
1461	
1462	section FamilyParameterized
1463	
1464	set_option linter.unusedSectionVars false
1465	
1466	variable {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1467	
1468	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → ι`.
1469	
1470	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
1471	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
1472	def coverInterOpenFam {κ : Type} (σ : κ → ι) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
1473	  ⨅ k, U (σ k)
1474	
1475	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
1476	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
1477	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
1478	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_leFam {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → ι) :
1479	    coverInterOpenFam U σ ≤ coverInterOpenFam U (σ ∘ α) :=
1480	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
1481	
1482	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
1483	
1484	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
1485	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)` and whose simplicial maps
1486	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
1487	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpenFam U σ ≤ coverInterOpenFam U (σ ∘ α)`.
1488	
1489	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplexFam` with `d² = 0` for
1490	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
1491	
1492	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
1493	Mathlib counterpart. -/
1494	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicialFam :
1495	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
1496	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)
1497	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
1498	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_leFam U α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
1499	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U τ))
1500	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
1501	  map_id n := by
1502	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
1503	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
1504	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
1505	    have eo : coverInterOpenFam U (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
1506	        = coverInterOpenFam U σ := by rw [e]
1507	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
1508	        (coverInterOpen_comp_leFam U (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
1509	      eqToHom_map]
1510	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
1511	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)) e
1512	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
1513	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
1514	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
1515	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
1516	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
1517	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
1518	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
1519	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
1520	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U τ)) e,
1521	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → ι =>
1522	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U τ)) e))
1523	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpenFam U) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
1524	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
1525	    congr 2
1526	
1527	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
1528	
1529	The chain complex `K(U)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
1530	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)`

1533	`cechFreeSimplicialFam`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
1534	
1535	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
1536	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplexFam :
1537	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
1538	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicialFam U)
1539	
1540	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of

1542	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
1543	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicialFam` by hand. -/
1544	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_XFam (p : ℕ) :
1545	    (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).X p
1546	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ) :=
1547	  rfl
1548	
1549	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
1550	`K(U)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
1551	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_U`. -/
1552	noncomputable def cechFreeAugFam :
1553	    (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
1554	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → ι => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpenFam U σ)
1555	
1556	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_U` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
1557	
1558	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(U)_0 ⟶ O_X`
1559	(`cechFreeAugFam`). Concretely `O_U(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member

1562	
1563	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
1564	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheafFam :
1565	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
1566	  Limits.image (cechFreeAugFam U)
1567	
1568	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(U)_1 ⟶ K(U)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same

1572	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_augFam (i : Fin 2) :
1573	    (cechFreeSimplicialFam U).δ i ≫ cechFreeAugFam U
1574	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → ι => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpenFam U σ)) := by
1575	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
1576	  intro σ
1577	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicialFam, cechFreeAugFam, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
1578	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
1579	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_leFam U _ σ)
1580	
1581	/-- The differential `K(U)_1 ⟶ K(U)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
1582	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
1583	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_augFam`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
1584	`K(U)_• ⟶ O_U[0]`. -/
1585	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_augFam :
1586	    (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAugFam U = 0 := by
1587	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).d 1 0
1588	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) 0 :=
1589	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) 0
1590	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
1591	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicialFam.obj` types — matching
1592	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_augFam`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
1593	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)

1595	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) 0
1596	      ≫ cechFreeAugFam U = 0 := by
1597	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
1598	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
1599	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_augFam, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_augFam]
1600	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
1601	    abel
1602	  rw [hd]; exact main

1604	/-- The differential `K(U)_1 ⟶ K(U)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf

1609	    (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAugFam U) = 0 := by
1610	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAugFam U)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
1611	    Limits.zero_comp]
1612	  exact cechFree_d_comp_augFam U
1613	
1614	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(U)_• ⟶ O_U[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).

1749	/-- **Drop-zeros isomorphism.** Evaluating the degree-`p` free Čech term at `V` and splitting off
1750	the vanishing summands: the full coproduct over all `σ : Fin (p+1) → ι` is isomorphic to the
1751	coproduct over the *surviving* multi-indices `{σ : V ≤ U_σ}`, because every non-surviving summand
1752	`(eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)` (with `V ≰ U_σ`) is a zero object
1753	(`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`).  This is the first of the two halves identifying the evaluated
1754	term with the combinatorial model. -/
1755	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalDropZerosFam
1756	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1757	    (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
1758	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1759	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ))) ≅
1760	      (∐ fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι // V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ} =>
1761	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1762	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U s.1))) where
1763	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => by
1764	    classical
1765	    exact if h : V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ then
1766	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι // V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ} =>
1767	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1768	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U s.1))) ⟨σ, h⟩
1769	    else 0
1770	  inv := Limits.Sigma.desc fun s =>
1771	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
1772	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1773	        (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ))) s.1
1774	  hom_inv_id := by
1775	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
1776	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id]
1777	    by_cases h : V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ
1778	    · rw [dif_pos h, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
1779	    · rw [dif_neg h, Limits.zero_comp]
1780	      exact ((freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le h).eq_zero_of_src _).symm
1781	  inv_hom_id := by
1782	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro s
1783	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, dif_pos s.2]

1785	/-- **Degreewise engine model of the evaluated free Čech term.** The degree-`p` term of the
1786	evaluated free Čech complex at `V` is isomorphic to the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
1787	`∐_{τ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  Built as the composite of
1788	`cechFreeEval_XFam` (evaluation commutes with the coproduct), `cechFreeEvalDropZerosFam` (discard the
1789	`V ≰ U_σ` summands), and `Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` along `survivingEquivFam` with the per-summand
1790	identification `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`.  This is the object half of the engine iso
1791	`cechFreeEvalEngineIsoFam`; the differential match remains. -/
1792	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalEngine_XFam
1793	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1794	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1795	        ((cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).X p) ≅
1796	      ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} =>
1797	        ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
1798	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
1799	  cechFreeEval_XFam U (Opposite.op V) p ≪≫ cechFreeEvalDropZerosFam U V p ≪≫
1800	    (Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv (survivingEquivFam U V p)
1801	      (g := fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι // V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ} =>
1802	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1803	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U s.1)))
1804	      (fun τ => freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le ((survivingEquivFam U V p τ).2))).symm
1805	
1806	/-- The degree-`p` object of the engine complex: `∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`. -/
1807	noncomputable abbrev cechEngineXFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1808	    ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
1809	  ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} => coverSectionModule V
1810	
1811	/-- The engine differential `C_{p+1} ⟶ C_p`: the alternating sum over `i : Fin (p+2)` of the
1812	coproduct reindexing maps `ι_σ ↦ (-1)^i • ι_{σ ∘ Fin.succAbove i}` that drop the `i`-th index of
1813	the multi-index `σ : Fin (p+2) → I₁(V)`.  Chain (insertion) dual of
1814	`FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`. -/
1815	noncomputable def cechEngineDFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1816	    cechEngineXFam U V (p + 1) ⟶ cechEngineXFam U V p :=
1817	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1818	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} => coverSectionModule V)
1819	      (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
1820	
1821	/-- Action of the engine differential on a coproduct injection: `ι_σ ≫ cechEngineDFam = ∑_i (-1)^i •
1822	ι_{σ ∘ succAbove i}`. -/
1823	lemma cechEngineD_ιFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ)
1824	    (σ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i}) :
1825	    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEngineDFam U V p
1826	      = ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1827	          Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} =>
1828	            coverSectionModule V) (σ ∘ i.succAbove) := by
1829	  simp only [cechEngineDFam, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]

1831	/-- **`d² = 0` for the engine complex**, via the same sign-reversing involution
1832	`(i, j) ↦ (i.succAbove j, j.predAbove i)` as `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_comp`, transported to
1833	the coproduct injections. -/
1834	lemma cechEngineD_compFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
1835	    cechEngineDFam U V (p + 1) ≫ cechEngineDFam U V p = 0 := by
1836	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
1837	  rw [Limits.comp_zero, ← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ιFam]
1838	  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
1839	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp, cechEngineD_ιFam, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
1840	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun q : Fin (p + 3) × Fin (p + 2) =>
1841	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (q.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (q.2 : ℕ)) •
1842	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} =>
1843	        coverSectionModule V) ((σ ∘ q.1.succAbove) ∘ q.2.succAbove))]
1844	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun q _ => (q.1.succAbove q.2, q.2.predAbove q.1))
1845	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
1846	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
1847	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
1848	      funext k
1849	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
1850	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
1851	    simp only [harg]
1852	    rw [← add_smul, FreeCechEngine.combSign_flip j i]
1853	    simp
1854	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
1855	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
1856	    intro hj
1857	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
1858	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
1859	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
1860	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
1861	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _

1863	/-- **The engine chain complex** `C•` of an open `V` meeting some cover member.
1864	
1865	The chain complex of `O_X(V)`-modules with `C_p = ∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)` and the
1866	alternating index-dropping differential `cechEngineDFam`.  It is the constant-coefficient combinatorial
1867	model that `cechFreeEvalEngineIsoFam` identifies with the evaluated free Čech complex; the nonempty
1868	acyclicity of the evaluated complex reduces to the contractibility of `C•`.
1869	
1870	Project-local: the constant-coefficient Čech model complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
1871	noncomputable def cechEngineComplexFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
1872	    ChainComplex (ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) ℕ :=
1873	  ChainComplex.of (cechEngineXFam U V) (cechEngineDFam U V) (cechEngineD_compFam U V)
1874	
1875	/-- The prepend-`i_fix` contracting map `C_p ⟶ C_{p+1}` of the engine complex: on the coproduct
1876	injection `ι_σ` (`σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`) it returns `ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}`. -/
1877	noncomputable def cechEnginePrependFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1878	    (i_fix : {i : ι // V ≤ U i}) (p : ℕ) :
1879	    cechEngineXFam U V p ⟶ cechEngineXFam U V (p + 1) :=
1880	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
1881	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} => coverSectionModule V)
1882	      (Fin.cons i_fix σ)

1884	/-- Action of the prepend map on a coproduct injection. -/
1885	lemma cechEnginePrepend_ιFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1886	    (i_fix : {i : ι // V ≤ U i}) (p : ℕ)
1887	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i}) :
1888	    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEnginePrependFam U V i_fix p
1889	      = Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} =>
1890	          coverSectionModule V) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) := by
1891	  simp only [cechEnginePrependFam, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]

1893	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id` for the engine complex in positive
1894	degree: the coproduct port of `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec`.  The `i = 0` term of `s ≫ d`
1895	returns the identity (dropping the just-prepended `i_fix`), and every higher term cancels in pairs
1896	against the corresponding term of `d ≫ s` by `FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ`. -/
1897	lemma cechEnginePrepend_specFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1898	    (i_fix : {i : ι // V ≤ U i}) (n : ℕ) :
1899	    cechEnginePrependFam U V i_fix (n + 1) ≫ cechEngineDFam U V (n + 1)
1900	      + cechEngineDFam U V n ≫ cechEnginePrependFam U V i_fix n
1901	      = 𝟙 (cechEngineXFam U V (n + 1)) := by
1902	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
1903	  rw [Preadditive.comp_add, Category.comp_id]
1904	  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEnginePrepend_ιFam, cechEngineD_ιFam]
1905	  rw [← Category.assoc, cechEngineD_ιFam, Preadditive.sum_comp]
1906	  simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp, cechEnginePrepend_ιFam]
1907	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun i : Fin (n + 3) => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1908	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (n + 2) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i} => coverSectionModule V)
1909	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (n + 3) → _) ∘ i.succAbove))]
1910	  have h0 : (Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (n + 3) → {i : ι // V ≤ U i})
1911	      ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 3)).succAbove = σ := by funext k; simp
1912	  rw [h0]
1913	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
1914	  rw [add_assoc, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
1915	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun k _ => by
1916	    rw [FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
1917	    abel), add_zero]

1919	/-- **Positive-degree exactness of the engine complex** (`Function.Exact` form), the coproduct port
1920	of `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_exact`.  When `I₁(V)` is nonempty (witnessed by `i_fix`), the
1921	engine differential is exact at every positive degree: a cycle `x` with `cechEngineDFam n x = 0` is the
1922	boundary `cechEngineDFam (n+1) (cechEnginePrependFam (n+1) x)`, by the contracting identity
1923	`cechEnginePrepend_specFam`.  Feeds the nonempty-case homology vanishing via
1924	`ModuleCat.shortComplex_exact`. -/
1925	lemma cechEngineD_exactFam (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
1926	    (i_fix : {i : ι // V ≤ U i}) (n : ℕ) :
1927	    Function.Exact (cechEngineDFam U V (n + 1)) (cechEngineDFam U V n) := by
1928	  intro x
1929	  constructor
1930	  · intro hx
1931	    refine ⟨(cechEnginePrependFam U V i_fix (n + 1)) x, ?_⟩
1932	    have hspec := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (cechEnginePrepend_specFam U V i_fix n)
1933	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_id] at hspec
1934	    have hx2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hspec x
1935	    rw [LinearMap.add_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.id_apply,
1936	      hx, map_zero, add_zero] at hx2
1937	    exact hx2
1938	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
1939	    have hcomp := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (cechEngineD_compFam U V n)
1940	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp, ModuleCat.hom_zero] at hcomp
1941	    have h2 := DFunLike.congr_fun hcomp y
1942	    rw [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearMap.zero_apply] at h2
1943	    exact h2

1949	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1950	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
1951	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj

1954	          (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
1955	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)) σ) := by

1957	      = Limits.sigmaComparison (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))
1958	          (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)) := rfl
1959	  rw [hinv]
1960	  exact Limits.ι_comp_sigmaComparison _ _ σ

1962	/-- **Reduced action of the engine identification on a surviving injection.** For a multi-index

1968	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι)
1969	    (hσ : V ≤ coverInterOpenFam U σ) :
1970	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
1971	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
1972	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ))) σ
1973	      ≫ (cechFreeEval_XFam U (Opposite.op V) p).inv ≫ (cechFreeEvalEngine_XFam U V p).hom

1984	/-- Action of the free Čech differential on a coproduct injection: the alternating sum of the

1987	private lemma cechFree_d_ιFam (p : ℕ) (σ : Fin (p + 2) → ι) :
1988	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U σ)) σ
1989	        ≫ (cechFreePresheafComplexFam U).d (p + 1) p
1990	      = ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1991	          (freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_leFam U i.succAbove σ))
1992	            ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U τ))
1993	                (σ ∘ i.succAbove)) := by
1994	  have main : Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ
1995	      ≫ AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) p
1996	      = ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
1997	          (freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_leFam U i.succAbove σ))
1998	            ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpenFam U τ))
1999	                (σ ∘ i.succAbove)) := by
2000	    refine (Preadditive.comp_sum Finset.univ (Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ)
2001	      (fun i : Fin (p + 2) => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (cechFreeSimplicialFam U).δ i)).trans ?_

2003	    intro i _
2004	    refine (Preadditive.comp_zsmul _ _ _).trans ?_
2005	    congr 1
2006	    show Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ (cechFreeSimplicialFam U).δ i = _
2007	    simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicialFam, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
2008	    rfl

2010	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) p from
2011	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicialFam U) p]

2021	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map

2030	/-- **Differential comm-square of the engine identification.** The degreewise object isos

2035	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :

2039	  refine (cancel_epi (cechFreeEval_XFam U (Opposite.op V) (p + 1)).inv).mp ?_

1345	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1346	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1347	
1348	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1349	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1350	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1351	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1352	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1353	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1354	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1355	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1356	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1357	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1358	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1359	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1360	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1361	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1362	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1363	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1364	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1365	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1366	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1367	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1368	\(\delta\)-functor identification.

1409	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1410	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
1411	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
1412	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
1413	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
1414	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
1415	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
1416	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
1417	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
1418	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
1419	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
1420	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
1421	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
1422	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
1423	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
1424	  \[
1425	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
1426	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1427	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
1428	  \]
1429	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
1430	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
1431	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
1432	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
1433	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
1434	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
1435	  \[
1436	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
1437	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1438	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1439	  \]
1440	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
1441	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
1442	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
1443	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
1444	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
1445	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
1446	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1447	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
1448	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
1449	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
1450	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
1451	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1452	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1453	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
1454	
1455	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
1456	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
1457	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
1458	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
1459	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
1460	\end{definition}

1499	  degree \(p\) is the product of sections over the \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,

1507	  differential the alternating sum of restriction maps
1508	  \[
1509	    d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1510	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
1511	        s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}
1512	        \big|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}.
1513	  \]

1515	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of

1521	  additive structure of the sections). The two must not be conflated: the
1522	  presheaf-level acyclicity argument below runs entirely on this section complex.

1592	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:section_cech_complex}
1593	  The free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules gives, composed with the
1594	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, \mathcal{F}) \cong
1595	  \mathcal{F}(V)\), the natural identification, for each multi-index,
1596	  \[
1597	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(
1598	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),\, \mathcal{F}
1599	    \bigr)
1600	    \;\cong\;
1601	    \operatorname{Hom}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p},\,
1602	      \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1603	    \;\cong\;
1604	    \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),
1605	  \]
1606	  where the last group is the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open
1607	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). Taking the product over multi-indices,
1608	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_p, \mathcal{F}) =
1609	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) =
1610	  \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\); the direct sum
1611	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) therefore represents the functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto
1612	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\). Under this
1613	  identification the \((-1)^j\) maps \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0
1614	  \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j}
1615	  \ldots i_{p+1}})\) defining the differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) become
1616	  exactly the alternating-sum restriction differential of
1617	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so the two cochain
1618	  complexes coincide; naturality in \(\mathcal{F}\) is immediate from the
1619	  naturality of the free–forgetful adjunction and of Yoneda. The per-multi-index
1620	  bijection \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,
1621	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}), \mathcal{F}) \cong \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\) is additive,
1622	  so it is promoted to an isomorphism of abelian groups (an \emph{additive}
1623	  equivalence); taking the product over multi-indices and assembling these degreewise
1624	  additive isomorphisms — which commute with the alternating-sum differentials — yields
1625	  the complex isomorphism by matching components degree by degree.
1626	\end{proof}

1652	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1653	  covering. The \emph{cover structure presheaf} \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} :
1654	  X.\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\) is the image presheaf of the augmentation
1655	  \[

1659	  X.\mathrm{ringCatSheaf}.\mathrm{obj})\) is the structure presheaf \(\mathcal{O}_X\)
1660	  regarded as a presheaf of modules over itself. Concretely
1661	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
1662	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise. The degree-\(0\) term
1663	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 = \coprod_i \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_i)\) of the free
1664	  presheaf complex of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} carries the
1665	  augmentation map \(K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) onto this image
1666	  presheaf.

1678	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1679	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1680	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1681	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1682	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1683	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1684	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1685	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1686	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1687	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1688	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1689	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1690	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1691	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1692	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1693	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1694	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1695	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1696	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1697	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1698	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1699	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1700	\end{lemma}
1701	
1702	\begin{proof}
1703	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1704	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1705	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1706	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1707	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1708	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1709	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1710	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1711	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1712	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1713	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1714	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1715	  quasi-isomorphism.
1716	\end{proof}

1718	\begin{lemma}
1719	
1720	[Sectionwise description of the evaluated free complex]
1721	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1722	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_X,
1723	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le,
1724	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le}
1725	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1726	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1727	  % (proof, the sectionwise description of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1728	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1227--1252).
1729	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
1730	  % if $i \in I_1$. ... If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1731	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$ and
1732	  % $K^{ext}_p(W) = \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W)$.
1733	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1734	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$."
1735	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1736	  (description of the evaluated complex).}
1737	  Fix an open \(V \subset X\) and write \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i \in I : V \leq U_i\,\}\)
1738	  for the set of cover members containing \(V\). Evaluating the free presheaf complex
1739	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} at
1740	  \(V\) gives, degreewise,
1741	  \[
1742	    K(\mathcal{U})_p(V)
1743	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I}
1744	        \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)(V)
1745	      = \bigoplus_{\substack{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I \\ V \leq U_\sigma}}
1746	        \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1747	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
1748	  \]
1749	  because \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1750	  \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise (poset hom-set is a subsingleton), and
1751	  \(V \leq U_\sigma = U_{\sigma(0)} \cap \cdots \cap U_{\sigma(p)}\) holds iff
1752	  \(\sigma\) takes all its values in \(I_1\). Under this identification the
1753	  differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) is the alternating-sum combinatorial
1754	  {\v C}ech differential of the full simplex on the index set \(I_1\) with
1755	  coefficients in the single module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Moreover the evaluated
1756	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) when
1757	  \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\) and \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\) when
1758	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\). Thus the evaluation at \(V\) of the augmented complex
1759	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
1760	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is precisely the augmented combinatorial
1761	  {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1762	  The Lean entry point for the degree-\(p\) unfolding is
1763	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) (which exposes
1764	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p = \coprod_\sigma \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)\) by
1765	  \(\operatorname{rfl}\)) composed with
1766	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\) at \(V\). The full degreewise
1767	  identification is assembled from three pieces: \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\)
1768	  (evaluation at \(V\) commutes with the coproduct, giving
1769	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong \coprod_\sigma
1770	  (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))\)),
1771	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\) (a surviving summand with
1772	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\) is \(\cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\)), and
1773	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\) (a summand with
1774	  \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\) is the zero object); collapsing the coproduct over the
1775	  surviving \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\) gives
1776	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
1777	\end{lemma}
1778	
1779	\begin{proof}
1780	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1781	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1782	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1783	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1784	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1785	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1786	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1787	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1788	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1789	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1790	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1791	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1792	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1793	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1794	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1795	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in
1796	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}.
1797	\end{proof}

1803	\begin{lemma}
1804	[Constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech contracting-homotopy engine]
1805	  \label{lem:free_cech_engine}
1806	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential,

1815	    AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero}
1816	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1817	  % (proof, the differential and contracting map of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1818	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1245--1268).
1819	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
1820	  % is given by
1821	  % $$
1822	  % d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
1823	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1824	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1825	  % $$
1826	  % The sum is finite as the element $s$ has finite support.
1827	  % Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1828	  % $$
1829	  % h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)
1830	  % $$
1831	  % by the rule
1832	  % $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1833	  % \left\{ \begin{matrix}
1834	  % 0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1835	  % s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}
1836	  % \end{matrix} \right.$"
1837	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1838	  (differential and contracting homotopy of the evaluated complex).}
1839	  Fix a nonempty index set \(J\) (in the application \(J = I_1(V)\)), a chosen index
1840	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in J\), and a single coefficient module \(M\). The
1841	  \emph{constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine} consists of: the
1842	  alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\) on the
1843	  full simplex over \(J\) with coefficients \(M\),
1844	  \[
1845	    (\operatorname{combDifferential} t)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1846	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, t_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}},
1847	  \]
1848	  which squares to zero, \(\operatorname{combDifferential} \circ
1849	  \operatorname{combDifferential} = 0\) (\(\operatorname{combDifferential\_comp}\)); the
1850	  \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} homotopy \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\),
1851	  \((\operatorname{combHomotopy}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, u)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1852	  = u_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}}\) when \(i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) and \(0\) otherwise; and
1853	  the contracting identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\)
1854	  (\(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_spec}\)), which yields positive-degree exactness
1855	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_exact}\) and the cocycle-splitting form
1856	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_eq\_of\_cocycle}\). The remaining declarations are
1857	  sign- and index-bookkeeping consumed by the contracting identity: the
1858	  succ-shift reindexing \(\operatorname{cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) and the
1859	  alternating-sign flip \(\operatorname{combSign\_flip}\), together with the
1860	  degenerate-case simplification \(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_zero}\). The generic
1861	  category-theory helper \(\operatorname{isZero\_sigma\_of\_forall\_isZero}\) (a
1862	  coproduct of zero objects is a zero object) is recorded here as well, used to collapse
1863	  the empty-index direct sums of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}. This engine is
1864	  self-contained combinatorics on \(\operatorname{Fin.cons}\),
1865	  \(\operatorname{Fin.succAbove}\), \(\operatorname{Fin.predAbove}\), and
1866	  \(\operatorname{Finset.sum\_involution}\); it mirrors the contracting homotopy of the
1867	  Stacks \texttt{lemma-homology-complex} proof.
1868	\end{lemma}

1914	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1915	  (the evaluated complex \(K^{ext}_\bullet(W)\) and its differential).}
1916	  Fix an open \(V\) with \(I_1(V) = \{\, i : V \leq U_i \,\} \neq \varnothing\), and
1917	  let \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) be the single coefficient module
1918	  (\(\operatorname{coverSectionModule}\)). The \emph{engine chain complex}
1919	  \(C_\bullet = \operatorname{cechEngineComplex}\, \mathcal{U}\, V\) is the chain
1920	  complex of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules with
1921	  \[
1922	    C_p \;=\; \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1923	    \qquad (\operatorname{cechEngineX}),
1924	  \]
1925	  the coproduct taken in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(\mathcal{O}_X(V))\), and with
1926	  differential \(d = \operatorname{cechEngineD}\) given on the injection
1927	  \(\iota_\sigma\) of the index \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)\) by the
1928	  alternating sum of index-drop reindexings,
1929	  \[
1930	    d \circ \iota_\sigma
1931	      \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{p} (-1)^i\, \iota_{\sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i}
1932	    \qquad (\operatorname{cechEngineD\_\iota}),
1933	  \]
1934	  i.e.\ the chain transpose of the index-\emph{raise} differential
1935	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) of
1936	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. Then:
1937	  \begin{itemize}
1938	    \item \(d \circ d = 0\) (\(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_comp}\)), so the family
1939	      assembles into the chain complex \(C_\bullet = \operatorname{ChainComplex.of}\)
1940	      (\(\operatorname{cechEngineComplex}\)).
1941	    \item The \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} map
1942	      \(s \mapsto \iota_{\operatorname{Fin.cons}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, \sigma}\)
1943	      (\(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\), with its injection computation
1944	      \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend\_\iota}\)) is a contracting homotopy in
1945	      positive degree: \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\)
1946	      (\(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend\_spec}\)).
1947	    \item Hence \(C_\bullet\) is exact in positive degree,
1948	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(\operatorname{cechEngineD}(n+1),
1949	      \operatorname{cechEngineD} n)\) (\(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\)).
1950	  \end{itemize}
1951	  Four object-half bricks realise the degreewise identification
1952	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong C_p\) consumed by
1953	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}: evaluation commutes with the coproduct;
1954	  the non-surviving summands (those \(\sigma\) with \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\)) drop to the
1955	  zero object (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\),
1956	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalEngine\_X}\)); and the surviving index type is reindexed
1957	  by the equivalence
1958	  \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv} : (\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)) \simeq
1959	  \{\, \sigma \;//\; V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma \,\}\),
1960	  where the membership criterion
1961	  \(V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma
1962	  \iff \forall k,\ V \leq U_{\sigma(k)}\) is
1963	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\).
1964	\end{lemma}

1969	  core of Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. The differential
1970	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) is defined on coproduct injections by the
1971	  alternating-sign index-drop \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove}
1972	  i\); both \(d \circ d = 0\) and the contracting identity
1973	  \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\) are proved by extending along the
1974	  injections with \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\) and reducing each
1975	  injection-component to the corresponding combinatorial cancellation of
1976	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential\_comp}\) respectively
1977	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\) (the prepend
1978	  \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\) is the coproduct dual of
1979	  \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\)). Positive-degree exactness
1980	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\) reads off the contracting identity. The four
1981	  object-half bricks are immediate: evaluation preserves \(\coprod\) and the surviving
1982	  summands are picked out by \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) under the criterion
1983	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\), the rest being zero objects
1984	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\)).
1985	\end{proof}
